user_query:
I'm a store manager trying to cut down manual lookups. Please build a small event-triggered automation that enriches any new deliveries CSV with ZIP code coordinates so I can estimate drive times for curbside orders.

Requirements:
- Watch the workspace directory "inbound/" for new files named "deliveries_*.csv". On startup, process any existing matching file(s) and then continue monitoring for new ones until the process is stopped.
- For each detected file, download the official U.S. Census Bureau 2020 Gazetteer Files dataset for ZIP Code Tabulation Areas (ZCTA) from the census.gov domain. Target the resource identified by file name "2020_Gaz_zcta_national.zip" (which contains a text file named "2020_Gaz_zcta_national.txt"). Use only that official source. Do not hardcode coordinates.
- Extract structured data from the downloaded archive by reading the ZCTA mapping of 5-digit codes to coordinates from the "2020_Gaz_zcta_national.txt" file. Use the fields: ZCTA5 (5-digit code), INTPTLAT, INTPTLONG. Build a mapping from ZIP to latitude/longitude using those columns.
- For each input row in the deliveries CSV (schema provided in inbound/deliveries_2026-04-15.csv), look up the 5-digit ZIP from the "zip" column. Append two new columns: "zip_lat" and "zip_lon". If a ZIP has no match in the ZCTA mapping, leave those new fields empty for that row.
- Save the enriched file as "out/<original_basename>_geocoded.csv" (e.g., inbound/deliveries_2026-04-15.csv -> out/deliveries_2026-04-15_geocoded.csv). Preserve original columns and add the two new columns at the end.
- Cache the downloaded source to make runs repeatable: write the original archive to "cache/2020_Gaz_zcta_national.zip" and the extracted text to "cache/2020_Gaz_zcta_national.txt". Also write a SHA-256 digest of the extracted text file to "cache/2020_gaz_source_sha256.txt" (hex string, one line). If the cached text file is missing or older than 30 days, re-download before processing.
- Append one JSON line per processed input file to "logs/processed.jsonl" with: {"input_file": str, "output_file": str, "processed_at": ISO8601 UTC, "input_rows": int, "matched_rows": int, "source_file_sha256": str}. Use this log to avoid reprocessing the same input file on subsequent runs.

Paths to use:
- Input folder: "inbound/" (sample included)
- Cache folder for the downloaded census files: "cache/"
- Output folder: "out/"
- Log file: "logs/processed.jsonl"

Deliverables created by your automation should include the enriched CSV in out/, the cached census files and hash in cache/, and the processing log in logs/. Please make sure your solution actually monitors the directory (not just a one-off script), downloads the census resource from the official domain by its file name, parses the structured text inside the ZIP, and produces the outputs as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
